title: The Strong Arms Of The Ma

jokes.text: Three divorces in three months. What can I say, Kent? I'm a romantic.
jokes.setup: Rainier filing for bankruptcy due to multiple divorces
jokes.punchline: Calling himself 'romantic' while describing rapid-fire divorces
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - only the absurdly romantic action star would frame multiple divorces as evidence of being romantic

jokes.text: This was done on her wedding day. Or should I say 'deading' day?
jokes.setup: Showing a painting of his grandmother on her wedding day
jokes.punchline: Dark pun about it being the day she died
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly predictable dark humor setup

jokes.text: How the mighty have fallen... into my price range. I wanna go there.
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting it with selfish excitement
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - starts philosophical then immediately reveals his true shallow motivation

jokes.text: Dad, we are there.
jokes.setup: Homer's excited declaration about wanting to go to the sale
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction that they're already there
jokes.explanation: Lisa as the straight man pointing out Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: Zip it, Holy Joe.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing moral concern about selling centerfolds
jokes.punchline: Dismissive nickname for the prudish housewife
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction with a quotable put-down

jokes.text: Miss September... you're gonna have to get in the trunk.
jokes.setup: Trying to fit purchases in the car
jokes.punchline: Casually addressing a centerfold like a person who needs transport
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise treated matter-of-factly, good visual comedy

jokes.text: Everything here is sword. Sword baseball bat, sword rifle, sword pineapple... sword sword sharpener, sword pie.
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly absurd sword combinations
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation comedy with 'sword sword sharpener' and 'sword pie' being genuinely absurd

jokes.text: Hey, what happened to the sword pie?
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely concerned about missing absurd item
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's priorities and obsessions

jokes.text: Hey, your early porno movies. Oh, wait, are any of these hetero?
jokes.setup: Finding adult movies at the sale
jokes.punchline: Casually asking inappropriate question about content
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of social awareness in embarrassing situations

jokes.text: The weights that changed you from a Bavarian cream puff... to a mass of twitching muscles?
jokes.punchline: Poetic description contrasting soft pastry with grotesque musculature
jokes.explanation: 'Bavarian cream puff' is a wonderfully specific and visual metaphor

jokes.text: They know you are talking about them.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the weights have consciousness and feelings
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd anthropomorphizing of gym equipment

jokes.text: This is what all those hours of playing Tetris were for.
jokes.setup: Need to fit purchases in car
jokes.punchline: Video game skills finally having real-world application
jokes.explanation: Relatable gaming reference with practical payoff

jokes.text: [Tetris theme music playing while Homer packs car]
jokes.punchline: Actual Tetris music accompanies the packing sequence
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual callback that makes the joke work on multiple levels

jokes.text: But there's no room for you. / D'oh!
jokes.setup: Successfully packing everything in the car
jokes.punchline: Realizing he packed himself out of transportation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer oversight - solving one problem while creating another

jokes.text: I used it to carry Rob Schneider in the movie My Baby is an Ugly Man.
jokes.setup: Offering Homer a giant baby carrier
jokes.punchline: Reference to absurd fake movie with Rob Schneider
jokes.explanation: Decent fake movie title parody, though not particularly clever

jokes.text: Your heartbeat is so soothing. / Shh. Time for sleep, little fatso.
jokes.setup: Homer being carried like a baby
jokes.punchline: Intimate moment between two grown men
jokes.explanation: Awkward intimacy comedy with masculine characters in baby situation

jokes.text: How did you turn cinnamon applesauce into that?
jokes.punchline: Incredulous question about transformation process
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor with mild cleverness

jokes.text: Don't try to pin this on me, sister.
jokes.punchline: Baby speaking in film noir style
jokes.explanation: Classic Maggie inner monologue with tough-guy attitude

jokes.text: The bathroom is not for customers. Please use the crack house.
jokes.setup: Marge asking for bathroom in emergency
jokes.punchline: Suggesting illegal drug den as alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd escalation of retail unfriendliness

jokes.text: That is the most pungent thing I've ever smelled and I am from India.
jokes.punchline: Using cultural background to emphasize severity
jokes.explanation: Uses Apu's character background effectively but relies on cultural stereotyping

jokes.text: That sign is just a ruse, like all high voltage signs.
jokes.punchline: Claiming all electrical safety warnings are fake
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist logic but not particularly character-specific

jokes.text: It's not a purse. It's a diaper bag. / It looks like a purse, that's why I bought it. / But it's really a--
jokes.punchline: Victim correcting criminal about accessory classification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - maintaining propriety even during a mugging

jokes.text: It's all my fault my wife got mugged. / There you have it, boys. Case closed.
jokes.punchline: Police immediately closing case based on confession to wrong crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - solving the wrong case with enthusiasm

jokes.text: Why is it Ned Flanders?
jokes.punchline: Questioning why neighbor is used as anatomical model
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about random character choice

jokes.text: Oh, like, I'm gonna kick Chief Wiggum in the groin.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's offended response about using himself as target
jokes.explanation: Decent character reaction showing Wiggum's ego

jokes.text: Even my boogers are spicy.
jokes.setup: Being accidentally pepper sprayed by Marge
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of uncomfortable sensation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph line - childish and innocent take on pain

jokes.text: Can you believe I get paid to wear short pants?
jokes.setup: Being run over by Marge's car
jokes.punchline: Focusing on job perks while injured
jokes.explanation: Mild workplace humor with misplaced priorities

jokes.text: I gave your necklace to my cheap girlfriend. I just throw it in a drawer at night. No, it'll tangle!
jokes.punchline: Marge more concerned about jewelry care than the insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Marge's domestic concerns override trauma

jokes.text: Oh, no, my glasses are broken. [Twilight Zone theme plays]
jokes.setup: Mailman trapped under car with reading material
jokes.punchline: Reference to classic Twilight Zone episode 'Time Enough at Last'
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that mirrors classic TV episode scenario exactly

jokes.text: It's called The Woman Who Died in Her Home.
jokes.punchline: Title suggests opposite of helpful treatment
jokes.explanation: Decent dark humor about unhelpful self-help media

jokes.text: I hope you like throwing dinner parties. / I do. / No one wants to eat dinner at a crazy lady's house. Get real.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting silver lining to agoraphobia
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with harsh reality
jokes.explanation: Good comedic reversal with Dr. Hibbert's blunt honesty

jokes.text: Don't worry. I'll set fire to the hive. / Twelve. Fifteen. Seven hundred and three.
jokes.punchline: Numbers escalating beyond scale due to Homer's 'help'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation comedy showing how Homer makes everything worse

jokes.text: I'm a magazine rack. / Look, I'm the first to admit it. I don't write good parts for women.
jokes.setup: Family therapy roleplay with Lisa as inanimate object
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking character to critique his own directing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about The Simpsons' own writing while maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: What play? What the hell is he talking about?
jokes.punchline: Homer completely forgetting his own therapeutic roleplay
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Homer's short attention span

jokes.text: We'll have to smoke her out.
jokes.setup: Kids asking what if mom doesn't get better
jokes.punchline: Treating wife like pest to be exterminated
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer logic treating family member like unwanted animal

jokes.text: Breaker, Bartman to shut-in. The big guy's asleep. Please advise. Over.
jokes.punchline: Military-style communication for domestic situation
jokes.explanation: Decent comedy from overly formal communication style

jokes.text: Why, you little-- Oh, ha, ha, hi, Jesus. I was just--
jokes.setup: Homer getting hit during prayer time
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly addressing Jesus mid-curse
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment of embarrassment caught between anger and piety

jokes.text: The air hockey table makes passing the carrots a breeze.
jokes.punchline: Finding practical use for game equipment
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about making the best of situation

jokes.text: I loved that show because you always knew... it was only a half-hour till Wings. Then you could just sleep till Monday.
jokes.punchline: Revealing he only watched Cheers to get to Wings, then sleep
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the point of beloved sitcom completely

jokes.text: If you don't, the oven's not on.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation about kitchen appliance
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that doesn't really land - too forced

jokes.text: I'm married to Joey Heatherton.
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific 1970s celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Dated reference that fits Homer's knowledge of obscure celebrities

jokes.text: I got all the way out to the tree before I realized I'd left the house.
jokes.punchline: Not realizing she'd conquered fear until after the fact
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment with gentle humor

jokes.text: Well, aren't you a super-duper recouper?
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic cheerful wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned-style wordplay - annoying but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Then you're not paying close enough attention.
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's pessimistic worldview as correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa line - world-weary wisdom delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: You've just been Marge-inalized.
jokes.setup: Marge defeating her mugger in fight
jokes.punchline: Action hero one-liner with her name
jokes.explanation: Good action movie parody with character-specific pun

jokes.text: It's like I'm married to Shaft.
jokes.punchline: Comparing wife to blaxploitation hero
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference fitting Homer's frame of reference

jokes.text: I have many anti-drug bumper stickers I'd be making a liar out of my tailgate.
jokes.punchline: Marge's concern about contradicting car decorations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge logic - more worried about hypocrisy than health risks

jokes.text: Steroids aren't drugs. They occur naturally in the body... like sweat or tumors.
jokes.punchline: Comparing steroids to increasingly disturbing natural things
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from benign to disturbing comparisons

jokes.text: Yes. Their main side effect is greatness.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly positive spin on drug risks
jokes.explanation: Good sales pitch parody with absurd optimism

jokes.text: Man, what am I smoking? Oh, yeah, pot.
jokes.setup: Otto confused about bus catching situation
jokes.punchline: Remembering his drug use explains confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto moment - self-aware about his drug use

jokes.text: I feel that illegal performance-enhancing drugs... are too common these days. Rebuttal?
jokes.punchline: Homer trying to turn romance into debate
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Homer avoiding intimacy through pedantry

jokes.text: I wasn't asking.
jokes.punchline: Marge's direct assertion of dominance
jokes.explanation: Perfect power reversal with maximum economy

jokes.text: Your mom has a lot of stuff to shave.
jokes.setup: Kids asking why mom didn't make lunch
jokes.punchline: Homer's uncomfortable explanation about steroid side effects
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable truth delivered in Homer's awkward way

jokes.text: Your mom has a lot of stuff to shave.
jokes.setup: Kids ask why mom didn't make lunch
jokes.punchline: Homer's explanation about steroid side effects
jokes.explanation: Homer's blunt explanation of uncomfortable reality

jokes.text: Ay caramba. I'm off women forever.
jokes.punchline: Extreme reaction to masculine-looking women
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart reaction with his catchphrase

jokes.text: I'm tired of her criticizing my saggy glutes. / Quiet. Her muscular ears can hear us.
jokes.punchline: Bart treating enhanced muscles like superpowers
jokes.explanation: 'Muscular ears' is genuinely absurd and funny concept

jokes.text: I didn't sacrifice my period for second place.
jokes.punchline: Blunt reference to steroid side effects
jokes.explanation: Shockingly frank Marge line about serious consequences

jokes.text: I don't got enough booze in this place to make you look good.
jokes.punchline: Moe's brutal honesty about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - insulting customer while admitting bar's limitations

jokes.text: Disco Stu should have disco ducked. Ow.
jokes.punchline: Character-appropriate disco pun about dodging
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific wordplay from one-note character

jokes.text: The woman who instead of swatting a fly... will give it a bath and send it on its way.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly gentle approach to pest control
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character description that's both funny and touching

jokes.text: Whoa, Moe, wait a minute. Don't you have to buy insurance first? / Oh, crap.
jokes.setup: Moe about to commit insurance fraud
jokes.punchline: Realizing he forgot the crucial first step
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff about incompetent criminal planning

jokes.text: Ready for a real workout, Marge? / Good. Can you wax the car? / Ow. Ow. Ow. I'm kidding. I'm kidding.
jokes.setup: Homer offering to work out together
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for domestic chores instead
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer trying to exploit situation for personal benefit

tmdb_overview: Marge gets mugged and becomes so traumatized that she develops Agoraphobia. She hides in the basement. Having little else to do, she decides to exercise with a weight set Homer bought from Rainier Wolfcastle's garage sale. She becomes so strong and confident that she is able to leave the basement. She continues working out and decides to start taking steroids so she can enter a bodybuilding competition. One night at Moe's Tavern she loses her temper and starts destroying the bar and fighting all of the patrons. Homer confronts Marge and says he misses the sweet woman that he married. Marge, horrified at what she has done, apologizes and decides to stop working out.
